summary: Any authenticated user can access the full user directory through the HTML and API user-list endpoints. This exposes all registered usernames, display names, and creation timestamps without any admin-only authorization check.

evidence_snippets.reason: The route name implies administrative access, but enforcement depends entirely on the controller.
The API exposes the same user-list operation.
Both handlers require only authentication and do not check any administrator role or equivalent authorization condition.

evidence_snippets.code:
$stmt = $this->pdo->query('SELECT user_id, username, display_name, created_at FROM users ORDER BY user_id ASC');
return $stmt->fetchAll();
evidence_snippets.reason: The repository returns the entire user directory to whichever authenticated caller reaches the endpoint.

root_cause: The user-list endpoints enforce only login status and omit any role-based authorization check, even though they expose global user records intended for an administrative context.
repair_plan: Introduce an explicit administrator authorization check before serving /admin/users and /api/users, and reject non-admin callers with a 403 response. If non-admin users need limited user data elsewhere, create separate endpoints or projections that return only the minimum permitted fields for that role.
